rag_rules: Match annex ownership to scope: type-scope contract content belongs on component types, while implementation-only bindings belong on implementations.
When fusing into an implementation, keep annex text after the relevant subcomponents and connections, and preserve all existing structural declarations.
Do not place references to subcomponent ports in a component-type annex unless those values are exposed through owner-visible names.
generic_rules: Select the owner by symbol visibility and scope, not by naming similarity or example structure.
Keep every inserted reference grounded in identifiers visible from the chosen owner.
Merge annex text without renaming, deleting, or reordering unrelated AADL elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE text must remain inside a valid `annex agree {** ... **};` block attached to the owning declaration.
Implementation-scoped annex content should follow the implementation's structural section rather than being embedded inside feature declarations.
anti_patterns: Do not move generated text into a broader or narrower owner if that change breaks identifier scope.
Do not let clause-generation examples or requirement patterns dictate fusion scope; use only owner/scope compatibility.
irrelevant_topics: Clause-level behavioral patterns such as conditional selection examples.
Requirement-generation heuristics for assumptions, guarantees, thresholds, or ranges.
Validator-specific advice about deleting unresolved AADL property-name expressions.
